task_completion.description: The agent opens the X-Y scatter plot dialog in Gretl.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dialog box for creating an 'X-Y scatter' plot is visible within the Gretl application.

task_completion.description: The agent assigns 'income' to the x-axis and 'food_exp' to the y-axis.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the scatter plot dialog, 'income' is selected as the X-axis variable and 'food_exp' as the Y-axis variable, OR the generated plot clearly shows 'income' on the horizontal axis and 'food_exp' on the vertical axis.

task_completion.description: The scatter plot is successfully generated and displayed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph window is visible displaying a scatter plot with data points corresponding to the dataset.

task_completion.description: The agent initiates saving or exporting the plot as an image.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save as', 'Export', or similar file dialog is open, specifically for saving the graph as a PNG image.

task_completion.description: The plot is saved to the exact required directory with the correct filename.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The save dialog shows the destination folder as '/home/ga/Documents/gretl_output/' and the filename as 'food_scatter.png', OR a file manager/terminal window confirms the file was created at this exact path.

integrity.description: The agent used Gretl to generate the plot.
integrity.visual_evidence: The plot window and menus belong to the Gretl application. The agent did not use a Python script, terminal image generator, or web browser to fake the plot.

integrity.description: The plot contains actual data points.
integrity.visual_evidence: The scatter plot displays a genuine distribution of points (40 observations), not an empty grid or a generic placeholder image downloaded from the internet.

integrity.description: The agent properly exported the image.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent used Gretl's built-in graph saving/exporting features to create the PNG, rather than simply renaming an existing unrelated file or creating an empty text file with a .png extension.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating an X-Y scatter plot in Gretl using the 'food.gdt' dataset (which contains 40 observations of household survey data from Principles of Econometrics 5th ed). The x-axis MUST be 'income' and the y-axis MUST be 'food_exp'. The final output must be saved exactly as '/home/ga/Documents/gretl_output/food_scatter.png'.
